title: Coprosma canthoides
taxonomy: Coprosma canthoides is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which includes a large and diverse group of flowering plants. This family is also known as the coffee or madder family, as it includes many species that have been used as sources of dyes and other products. Coprosma canthoides is a species of flowering plant in the Rubiaceae family and is native to New Zealand. The plant is also known as the "mirror bush" or "looking glass plant" due to its shiny leaves. It is a shrub or small tree that is commonly found in coastal areas and is valued for its ornamental foliage.
uses: Coprosma canthoides is native to New Zealand, and while it is primarily known for its ornamental value, there is limited information available on its medicinal uses. properties: Coprosma canthoides, also known as the New Zealand spinach coprosma or the coastal coprosma, is a plant that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. constituents: Coprosma canthoides contains active constituents such as alkaloids, particularly scopoletin, which is a furanocoumarin compound.
